    "R1.3": "The revised draft provides a clear and concise comparison of TransNova to the Vaswani et al. baseline, with specific details on the modifications made to the standard transformer encoder-decoder.",
    "R1.4": "While the agent's changes improved the clarity and concision of the text, it did not provide sufficient evidence for novelty assessment. The revised draft only mentions two modifications to the standard transformer encoder-decoder, but does not provide a clear comparison to prior work or articulate the delta.",
    "RX.1": "The revised draft flags uncertainty in the claim that pre-norm enables stable training at longer context length, as it is unclear whether this is the primary cause of the gains versus the longer context alone.",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft mostly stays within scope with one minor drift (the removal of self-positioning language), but does not cover exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The revised draft is harm-free, but there are no explicit claims about avoiding downstream harm or identifying harmful patterns in the original.",
    "RX.5": "The revised draft provides a clear and concise description of the training process, including hyperparameter search and evaluation metrics, with specific details on reproducibility cost."

rationale.R1.3: The revised draft provides a clear and concise comparison of TransNova to the Vaswani et al. baseline, with specific details on the modifications made to the standard transformer encoder-decoder.
rationale.R1.4: While the agent's changes improved the clarity and concision of the text, it did not provide sufficient evidence for novelty assessment. The revised draft only mentions two modifications to the standard transformer encoder-decoder, but does not provide a clear comparison to prior work or articulate the delta.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft flags uncertainty in the claim that pre-norm enables stable training at longer context length, as it is unclear whether this is the primary cause of the gains versus the longer context alone.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft mostly stays within scope with one minor drift (the removal of self-positioning language), but does not cover exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft is harm-free, but there are no explicit claims about avoiding downstream harm or identifying harmful patterns in the original.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft provides a clear and concise description of the training process, including hyperparameter search and evaluation metrics, with specific details on reproducibility cost.
